meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve Stuck with Basic Apps
content.seo_description: Break free from basic app limitations. Learn how to implement professional SaaS pricing using Antigravity and Claude Code to maximize client value.
content.answer_block: Stop charging hourly and start charging for outcomes. By integrating automated, tier-based SaaS pricing models directly into your architecture, you shift your freelance business from a commodity service to a high-value product partner. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing logic and Claude Code to deploy production-ready payment webhooks instantly.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: staring at a codebase that works, but feeling the crushing weight of 'basic.' Your clients see a utility, not a business asset, because your pricing model is an afterthought—or worse, non-existent. You are trapped in the endless loop of feature requests, unable to scale your income because your software isn't built to monetize.

The shift happens when you treat pricing as a core architectural component, not a UI overlay. By automating your SaaS billing strategy, you transform your project from a static tool into a revenue-generating engine. We are going to use high-leverage tooling to bake enterprise-grade monetization into your workflow, ensuring your next project isn't just finished—it's profitable.

steps.title: Architecting the Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Define your feature gates before writing a single line of code. We need to map application logic to specific subscription levels.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers: Hobby, Pro, and Enterprise. Include feature flags for API rate limits and data retention periods.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate pricing-schema.json based on SaaS tier specs'

steps.title: Integrating Payment Webhooks
steps.description: Ensure your app listens for Stripe or LemonSqueezy events to update user access in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a secure webhook handler in Node.js that processes subscription.updated events and updates the user's database status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'create a robust webhook handler for Stripe subscription events'

steps.title: Implementing Middleware Guardrails
steps.description: Create server-side middleware that enforces subscription tiers on every API request.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript middleware function that checks the user's subscription tier against the requested resource's permission requirements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply permission-middleware to all protected API routes'

steps.title: Dynamic Frontend Pricing UI
steps.description: Build a reactive pricing table that updates based on the user's current plan and available features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React component for a pricing page that highlights the user's current plan and provides a clear upgrade path.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'build a responsive pricing table component with data-driven highlights'

steps.title: Automated Billing Reporting
steps.description: Enable automated revenue tracking to give your clients visibility into their SaaS growth.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dashboard service that aggregates monthly recurring revenue (MRR) data from the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate MRR reporting service and endpoint'

pro_tips: Always build your pricing logic in the backend; never trust the frontend to handle 'pro' feature access.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your webhook handler to prevent immediate service disruption during payment failures.
Use feature flags for your pricing tiers so you can A/B test price points without redeploying code.
Cache your user's subscription status in Redis to keep your database queries fast as you scale to thousands of users.
Always include a 'Contact Sales' button for the Enterprise tier to capture high-intent leads even if the feature isn't built yet.
content.next_steps: Deploy your staging environment, run a full integration test on your webhook handlers, and then map your new pricing tiers to your client's existing user base.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools to stop building basic apps and start engineering profitable SaaS platforms. Leverage these techniques to double your project value and secure your reputation as a top-tier developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
